ORDER AND STIPULATION TO CONTINUE HEARING ON DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-03-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ASHBURY GENERAL CONTRACTING & ENGINEERING, INC., ANTHONY BYRNE AND KEVIN BORN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DUTY TO "ACT IN A REASONABLE MANNER" ARISES FROM THE CONTRACT. (COMPLAINT, PARS. 13, 20.) PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE A DUTY THAT DOES NOT ARISE FROM CONTRACT. (SEE AAS V. SUP. CT. (2000) 24 CAL.4TH 627, 643, "A PERSON MAY NOT ORDINARILY RECOVER IN TORT FOR THE BREACH OF DUTIES THAT MERELY RESTATE CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATIONS.") AS TO THE INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION, A FRAUD COMPLAINT MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT "SHOW HOW, WHEN, WHERE, TO WHOM, AND BY WHAT MEANS THE REPRESENTATIONS WERE TENDERED." (LAZAR V. SUP. CT. (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 631, 645.) PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION WITH THE REQUISITE AMOUNT OF SPECIFICITY. AS TO THE VIOLATION OF BUS. & PROF. CODE SEC. 17200 CAUSE OF ACTION, BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGED A FRAUDULENT BUSINESS PRACTICE, HE HAS NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGED UNFAIR COMPETITION. MR. JUNGINGER TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAR-04-2015 CONTINUED TO APR-15-2015 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ASHBURY GENERAL CONTRACTING & ENGINEERING, INC., ANTHONY BYRNE, AND KEVIN BORN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE, SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSPIRACY, AND OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF SECTION 17200 AND THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION. THE NEGLIGENCE CLAIM STILL FAILS TO ALLEGE A DUTY THAT DOES NOT ARISE FROM THE PARTIES' CONTRACT. WHILE PLAINTIFFS HAVE PREVIOUSLY BEEN GIVEN AN OPPORTUNITY TO CURE THIS DEFICIENCY AND FAILED TO DO SO, THEY NOW HAVE COUNSEL WHO MIGHT BE ABLE TO CURE THE DEFICIENCY. CONSPIRACY IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION, BUT RATHER A MEANS OF IMPUTING LIABILITY FOR A CAUSE OF ACTION. LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, THE 17200 AND MISREPRESENTATION CLAIMS ADEQUATELY PLEAD AN ACTIONABLE FRAUDULENT BUSINESS PRACTICE AND PROMISSORY FRAUD RESPECTIVELY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/HEK)

Defendants Ashbury General Contracting & Engineering, Inc., Anthony Byrne And Kevin Born'S Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to Defendants Ashbury General Contracting & Engineering, Inc., Anthony Byrne, and Kevin Born's Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint, Defendants' demurrer to the first cause of action for nuisance is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Defendants are granted leave to allege a nuisance claim that focuses solely on defendants' work at the property adjacent to their property and which deletes any allegation regarding defendants' work at plaintiff's property. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-28-2015 Continued To Nov-25-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ASHBURY GENERAL CONTRACTING & ENGINEERING, INC., ANTHONY BYRNE, AND KEVIN BORN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for negligence, sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for conspiracy, and overruled as to the fourth cause of action for violation of section 17200 and the fifth cause of action for intentional misrepresentation. The negligence claim still fails to allege a duty that does not arise from the parties' contract. While plaintiffs have previously been given an opportunity to cure this deficiency and failed to do so, they now have counsel who might be able to cure the deficiency. Conspiracy is not a cause of action, but rather a means of imputing liability for a cause of action. Liberally construed, the 17200 and misrepresentation claims adequately plead an actionable fraudulent business practice and promissory fraud respectively. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-03-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANTS ASHBURY GENERAL CONTRACTING & ENGINEERING, INC., ANTHONY BYRNE and KEVIN BORN'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Off calendar as a duplicate entry.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-03-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARY MCNEILL, TEL. NO. 223-6123 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ASHBURY GENERAL CONTRACTING & ENGINEERING, INC., ANTHONY BYRNE and KEVIN BORN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. As to the negligence cause of action, the duty to "act in a reasonable manner" arises from the contract. (Complaint, pars. 13, 20.) Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege a duty that does not arise from contract. (See Aas v. Sup. Ct. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 627, 643, "A person may not ordinarily recover in tort for the breach of duties that merely restate contractual obligations.") As to the intentional misrepresentation cause of action, a fraud complaint must plead facts that "show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege intentional misrepresentation with the requisite amount of specificity. As to the violation of Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200 cause of action, because Plaintiff has not adequately alleged a fraudulent business practice, he has not adequately alleged unfair competition. Mr. Junginger to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
